zh.lifeSummary: 當Christian Saint Quintin於1295年出生在英國越可爾時，她的父親William St. Quintin36歲，母親Agnes Heslarton36歲。她於1321年與Oliver Woodruff在英國越可爾伍利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1391年在英國越可爾哈法姆享年96歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Christian Saint Quintin于1295年出生在英国越可爾时，她的父亲William St. Quintin36岁，母亲Agnes Heslarton36岁。她于1321年与Oliver Woodruff在英国越可爾伍利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1391年在英国越可爾哈法姆享年96岁。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Quintin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Yves, Andre, Dominique, Gilles, Lucien, Michel, Oliva, Roch.
Breton: from the personal name Quintin, which is of the same Latin origin as 2 below, but also considered a derivative of Old Breton kint ‘first’.
French; Spanish (Quintín): from the personal name Quintin (Spanish Quintín), from Latin Quintinus, a Christian saint's name (see Quentin ).

variants.name: Quinton
meanings: English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Quentin, Quintin (Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, and brought to England by the Normans. The surname was altered to Quinton by association with the placenames in 2 below.

English: habitational name from from any of three places called Quinton (Gloucestershire, Northamptonshire, Worcestershire) or from Quainton (Buckinghamshire). The placenames probably derive from Old English cwēn ‘queen’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, or perhaps from Old English cwene ‘woman’ + tūn or an Old English personal name Cwēna + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Quentin (Manche) in France or possibly from Saint-Quentin-en-Tourmont (Somme), the site of the martyrdom of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. It is not certain that this surname survived into the modern period.

variants.name: Quentin
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Quentin (from Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. Compare Quintin .
English: variant of   Quinton  .

nameMeanings.name: Christian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

variants.name: Christianne
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Christian Saint Quintin was born in 1295, in Yorkshire, England, her father, William St. Quintin, was 36 and her mother, Agnes Heslarton, was 36. She married Oliver Woodruff in 1321, in Woolley, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. She died in 1391, in Harpham, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 96.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Christian Saint Quintin est née en 1295, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William St. Quintin, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Agnes Heslarton, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Oliver Woodruff en 1321, à Woolley, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1391, à Harpham, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 96 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Christian Saint Quintin 1295 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William St. Quintin 36 und ihre Mutter Agnes Heslarton 36. Sie hat 1321 in Woolley, Yorkshire, England Oliver Woodruff geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1391 im Alter von 96 Jahren in Harpham, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Christian Saint Quintin è nata il nel 1295 nel Yorkshire, England, sua padre, William St. Quintin, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Agnes Heslarton, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Oliver Woodruff nel 1321, nel Woolley, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1391, nel Harpham, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 96 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Christian Saint Quintinが1295年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父William St. Quintinは36歳，母Agnes Heslartonは36歳であった。1321年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウリーでOliver Woodruffと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1391年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハーファムで96歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Christian Saint Quintin(이)가 1295년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William St. Quintin(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Heslarton(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1321년 영국 요르크실르 보리에서 Oliver Woodruff(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1391년 영국 요르크실르 하르프아에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Christian Saint Quintin nasceu em 1295, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William St. Quintin, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Heslarton, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Oliver Woodruff em 1321, em Woolley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1391, em Harpham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 96 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Christian Saint Quintin родилась в 1295 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William St. Quintin было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Heslarton, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Oliver Woodruff в 1321 г. в местности вулли, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1391 г. в местности харфам, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 96 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Christian Saint Quintin nació en 1295, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William St. Quintin, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Agnes Heslarton, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Oliver Woodruff en 1321, en Woolley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas. Murió en 1391, en Harpham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 96 años.
